item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.1 Index, List of Drawings, 1953
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
UNIVERSITY OF UTAH LIBRARIES
TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE
U.S.A. ELECTS PRESIDENT
TOKYO - FUJI - KYOTO
VARIATIONS ON THE THEME G.B.S.
Bridges Arch 158 Sutton Walk LONDON S.E.1
sketch_descriptions: A tall man in a suit and bowler hat stands with one hand in his pocket and the other holding a cane, drawn in expressive, fluid lines.
A small sketch of a figure in profile carrying a bag or bundle.
Portrait of a man's head in profile view.
A scene showing multiple people near traditional Japanese architecture, with Mount Fuji visible.
A group of figures gathered closely together, heads bowed and faces expressive, evocative of a crowd or mass scene.
A portrait of an elderly man with distinct features.
A smaller full-body figure walking or standing, partially framed by vertical lines.
A block of multiple vignettes, likely depicting a series of people engaged in discussion or social activity.
pages.page_summary: This page of Topolski's Chronicle features a large, expressive portrait of a man in formal attire alongside a series of smaller vignettes capturing moments from political and cultural events, including the U.S. presidential election and scenes from Japan. Visual motifs reference George Bernard Shaw and social gatherings, reflecting Topolski's documentary style.

pages.typeset_text:
Each issue of Topolski's Chronicle measures 18 by 23 inches. Published 24 times a year at Bridge Arch 158, Sutton Walk, South Bank, London, S.E.1, England. Annual subscription (with folder and index) is $12.00 (£4 8s or equivalent). The complete collection is still available: 1953 volume of 12 issues $9.00 (£3 3s); 1954, 1955, 1956, 1957, 1958, 1959 & 1960 volumes of 24 issues $12.00 (£4 4s) each.

JOYCE CARY: ...The most brilliant record we have of the contemporary scene as lived by a contemporary mind. ANDREW RITCHIE of the NEW YORK MUSEUM MODERN ART: ...Today Topolski has perhaps no equal in pictorial journalism. JOHN ROTHENSTEIN-Director of the TATE GALLERY, London: ...We should ban the stiff, able and faithful school of representational art in favour of such as his. Interest in the whole art lies as much in the script with which he writes, as in his drawing... He is kin to Daumier and the Impressionists—perhaps he is even the Impressionists, parading in black armlets and laughing. THE TIMES (London): ...One of the most remarkable records of our time that exists in graphic form. ANDRE MALRAUX: ...la version moderne de la Comedie Humaine. TÉMOIGNAGE CHRÉTIEN (Paris): ...Le chroniqueur Topolski a une conscience de l'évènement supérieure à celle des plus grands reporters... CARREFOUR (Paris): ...the greatest Chronicle from London. Chronicles from all the capitals would be a real service to culture. CITÉ (France): ...Topolski est semblable à certains visionnaires du Moyen Age ...GRAFIS (Zurich): ...the line sometimes near to madness; the sight of expression is not always secure; especially grotesque scenes are rendered with such a boldness of expression as one never sees. L'AURORE (Paris): ...the Topolski Chronicle is admirable for its amplitude and for its movement. IRISH TIMES (Dublin): ...his book contains as little conventional reportage as trampolining and chess have of one another. ART DIRECTION (New York): ...You can sense with throbbing vividness the heart and the shape of our contemporary world. (Museum of Modern Art, New York). ...When has there been an artist before, either in or out of the pages of Life and Picture Post, to go out into the world and observe the things you see walking behind the changing banners of the street: HEAD-LINE FROM ARTE (Bologna, Italy): ...Topolski is a Comédie Humaine.
sketch_descriptions: A group scene of several people walking together, rendered in expressive, fast line work.
A motley group of figures beneath the handwritten 'TOPOLSKIS KRONIKE', suggesting a scene of everyday life or social observation.
A small vignette of figures among columns of what appears to be Hebrew text, with one figure standing and others in a rural setting.

places: Bridge Arch 158, Sutton Walk, South Bank, London, S.E.1, England
New York Museum of Modern Art
events: Coronation of H.M. Queen Elizabeth II

pages.page_summary: This page features editorial reviews and global press commentary showcasing the critical acclaim of Topolski's Chronicle, along with an index of volumes and issues from 1953 to 1960 and several expressive sketches typical of Topolski's reportage style.

pages.page_summary: This page is the index for the first 1953 volume of Topolski's Chronicle, listing the subjects and scenes illustrated throughout the year. It documents a range of events, locations, notable people, and political or cultural moments across Europe and beyond.

pages.page_summary: This index page from Topolski's Chronicle 1953 Volume I provides a comprehensive reference to the drawings within, listing people, places, and notable events depicted in the volume. It serves as a retrieval guide to the chronicle’s visual documentation of political, cultural, and social moments in 1953.
